title: Minuartia rostrata
intro: Minuartia rostrata, commonly known as Beaked Bellflower, is a low-growing, perennial alpine plant native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Minuartia rostrata include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal preparations, Minuartia rostrata is often combined with other plants to make topical creams, salves, and infusions to promote wound healing and soothe skin irritations.
taxonomy: Minuartia rostrata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is a part of the genus Minuartia, which includes various small annual and perennial plants.
